# @zakkster/lite-headless/tooltip

createTooltip(options) -> TooltipHandle

## Options

Inherits positioning options from createPopover. Additional:

- trigger: string = 'hover focus'        -- space-separated subset of 'hover|focus|click|manual'
- openDelay: number = 200                -- ms before opening on hover (ignored for focus)
- closeDelay: number = 150               -- ms before closing on leave (ignored for focus)
                                            -- this is the trigger->content gap grace period
- describesTrigger: boolean = true       -- true: aria-describedby; false: aria-labelledby

Default placement: 'top'. Default offset: 6.

No focus trap (tooltips never trap). closeOnEscape:true by default.

## Trigger model

Pointer state tracking: closes only when pointer has left BOTH trigger AND content.
Allows the user to move pointer from trigger into the tooltip content (e.g. to
click a link) without dismissal.

Focus triggers open/close synchronously (no delays) for keyboard accessibility.

## Returned

{
    open, status, setOpen, toggle,
    attachTrigger(el),    -- binds pointerenter/leave + focus/blur + click per trigger spec
    attachAnchor(el),     -- (optional) positioning origin distinct from trigger
    attachContent(el),    -- gets role="tooltip"
    attachArrow(el),
    destroy(),
    destroyed
}

## Slot conventions (for <lite-tooltip>)

- [data-trigger]: bound to hover/focus/click per `trigger` attribute
- [data-anchor]:  (optional) positioning anchor distinct from trigger
- [data-content]: gets role="tooltip"
- [data-arrow]:   (optional) arrow element

## <lite-tooltip> attributes

- open                                    -- reflects state, reactive
- placement, offset, no-flip, no-shift    -- positioning
- trigger                                 -- e.g. 'hover focus'
- open-delay, close-delay                 -- ms
- labels                                  -- describesTrigger:false (use aria-labelledby)
- no-escape, container, transition

## ARIA wiring

- content: role="tooltip", id auto-assigned if absent
- trigger: aria-describedby (default) or aria-labelledby pointing at content.id

## CSS contract

This primitive's painted attributes follow the canonical CSS contract
in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. See that doc for the cross-primitive
taxonomy (booleans like `data-open` / `data-disabled` / `data-loading`,
enums like `data-status` / `data-side`, ARIA pairings, host accessor
naming, and event naming).
